summary: Connected progress bars directly to pagination items using ::after pseudo-elements instead of separate div
userRevisions: Remove separate progress bar div below pagination
Add bottom bars to each pagination item using ::after pseudo-elements
Gray bar for all items by default, gradient bar for current/active item
Bars should be flush/connected to bottom of each item (no gap)
Keep separate from hover circle border effect

changes.description: Added ::after pseudo-elements to .page-numbers for bottom bars (4px gray by default, gradient for .current), positioned 6px below each item, no bar for .dots
Added box-shadow to .prev/.next for bottom bars (using box-shadow instead of ::after since those are already used for arrow icons)
Removed separate progress bar div (search-pagination-progress) and its inline --progress custom property
Removed all progress bar CSS rules (.search-pagination-progress and child div styles)

implementation.separation: independent from hover circle border effect

technicalNotes: Each .page-numbers now has position: relative to anchor ::after pseudo-element
::after creates bottom bar positioned absolutely 6px below item (bottom: -6px)
Default bar: 4px tall, full width, gray rgba(31,31,29,0.5), rounded 10px
Current page bar: same dimensions but gradient background (neon-green → blue)
Dots have ::after { display: none } to hide bottom bar
Prev/next arrows use box-shadow: 0 10px 0 -6px rgba(31,31,29,0.5) for bottom bar
Box-shadow on arrows avoids conflict with ::before/::after arrow icons
Hover circle border on page numbers does not affect ::after positioning
Removed separate search-pagination-progress div from PHP markup
Removed related CSS rules from Search.css
No JavaScript changes needed (no JS was handling progress bar)

verification.accessibility: maintained (ARIA attributes, keyboard support, screen reader announcements)
verification.visualIntegrity: bottom bars now connected/flush to pagination items, current item shows gradient
